name: authority_cleanup_dispatch_follow_ons
outcome_goal: Close the remaining dispatch authority-cleanup follow-ons so cancellation truth and dispatch setup ownership are both explicit, durable, and shared across CLI, MCP, and API front doors.
anti_requirements: Do not leave cancellation success dependent on best-effort status updates.
Do not preserve parallel setup factories as hidden authority seams.
Do not widen into unrelated workflow discovery or provider-default cleanup.

jobs.label: dispatch_cancellation_contract
jobs.prompt: Make cancellation one canonical contract. Cancel requests must converge on the same durable state transition, error shape, and operator-visible proof no matter whether they arrive through CLI, MCP, or API. Fail closed when claimed work cannot be cancelled safely; do not emit fake success.

jobs.label: dispatch_setup_authority_split
jobs.prompt: Collapse dispatch setup and factory assembly onto one deterministic authority boundary. The repo front doors may stay thin wrappers, but setup, argument shaping, and dispatch-factory ownership cannot remain split across bootstrap helpers and surface-specific glue.

jobs.label: dispatch_follow_on_proof
jobs.prompt: Prove the follow-ons instead of hand-waving them closed. Cover durable cancellation outcomes, surface parity across CLI/MCP/API, and the absence of hidden setup/factory fallbacks.
